LHX.sentence: L3Harris bounced back up about 3% today, recovering some ground from being down nearly 18% from its recent high — defense spending remains steady but it still trades cheaper than the bigger contractors.

ASML.sentence: ASML surged almost 10% today and is now running about 24% above its recent average, which is getting stretched even for a company that's the only supplier of the world's most advanced chipmaking machines.

LIN.sentence: Linde is having a quiet day with corporate credit markets staying stable, which tends to support the long-term industrial projects that drive demand for their gases.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft is down nearly 30% from its high despite having rock-solid profit margins, which is unusual punishment for a company at the center of the AI boom.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher is trading at attractive levels compared to its own history, and the bioprocessing equipment cycle that's been hurting them appears to be bottoming out.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge is basically flat today but its dividend yield has gotten very close to government bond rates, which historically means it's gotten expensive relative to the safety it offers.

NOW.sentence: ServiceNow had no data available today.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore fell about 2% while copper prices surged 7%, creating an unusual gap where the commodity is racing ahead of one of its biggest miners.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin is up about 3% today but still sitting 28% below its moving average and close to its recent low, making it look beaten down after the big run-up earlier this year.

KOF.sentence: Coca-Cola FEMSA rose about 3% today with the Mexican peso holding steady, which removes one of the big headwinds for this Latin American bottler.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras is up modestly with oil at $95, but its earnings look so high right now that it suggests we might be near a peak in the oil cycle.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk jumped nearly 5% today and is now trading below the book value of its ships and infrastructure, which often signals a good deal in the cyclical shipping business.
